journal_entry.rationale: Inventory is acquired as an asset (increases asset account). Since purchased on account, creates a liability (Accounts Payable increases). The transaction appears to be for inventory in a retail or manufacturing context. No tax information was provided in the lookup results, so no tax lines are added. Debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) requires 1 asset increase line; credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) requires 1 liability increase line.

output.reason: Purchasing inventory on account means we received goods but will pay later. The credit side records the obligation to pay = accounts payable = liability increase

credit_classifier.reason: Purchasing inventory on account means we received goods but will pay later. The credit side records the obligation to pay = accounts payable = liability increase

output.reason: Purchase inventory on account: Inventory is an asset account. When inventory is purchased, it increases the asset. Under IFRS, inventory is debited to increase it. This is 1 asset increase line.
